name: pr_tms_cycle
drl:
rule RM when Ord(v > 0) then insertLogical(new Mid("m")); end
rule RML when Mid(k == "m") then insertLogical(new Leaf("l")); end
rule RLM when Leaf(k == "l") then insertLogical(new Mid("m")); end
_finding: The logical-insert CYCLE (external review round 17, the well-foundedness cell): Mid justifies Leaf, Leaf justifies Mid (the back-edge merges onto Mid's key as a second justifier). Delete the grounded root and BOTH SURVIVE on circular mutual support - permanently, with no external handle to break the loop. Drools' TMS is justification-set based (JTMS), NOT well-founded: it never asks whether support is grounded, so the cycle orphans in both engines identically. The certified contract: insertLogical cycles produce permanently-unreclaimable facts; authors must keep logical derivation DAG-shaped (the acyclic control pr_tms_cycle_ctl cascades correctly).
